title: andruris japonica
intro: Andruris japonica, commonly known as Japanese quince, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Andruris japonica include pain relief, fever reduction, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These bioactive compounds contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic effects, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties.
parts: Andruris japonica has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, rhizomes, and leaves, which are used in traditional Chinese medicine. The roots of Andruris japonica are rich in andrographolide and andrographiside, two compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The rhizomes of the plant contain compounds like andrographolone and 14-deoxyandrographolone, which have been found to have anti-cancer and antioxidant effects. The leaves of Andruris japonica contain flavonoids like andrographin and andrographin B, which have been reported to have immunomodulatory and hepatoprotective activities.
preparations: Andruris japonica has many herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used for various medicinal purposes. Tea made from the leaves of Andruris japonica is often used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of anxiety. The tincture, on the other hand, is commonly used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for individuals experiencing menstrual cramps and arthritis. Additionally, the plant's extracts are sometimes used to create topical creams and salves, which are applied directly to the skin to treat wounds and skin irritations.
side_effects: Andruris japonica can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high toxicity levels. Prolonged consumption can also lead to liver and kidney damage, potentially causing conditions like jaundice and acute kidney failure. Furthermore, improper use of Andruris japonica can trigger allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals. In severe cases, incorrect administration can even result in seizures, coma, and even death.
